text.title: Emotion CSS-in-JS
text.summary: Emotion css prop requires jsxImportSource pragma or Babel plugin. Composition via css() objects. Emotion-specific gotchas for strict TypeScript and App Router.

rule.statement: Use the @emotion/babel-plugin for automatic jsxImportSource injection. Without it, add /** @jsxImportSource @emotion/react */ at the top of every file that uses the css prop.
rule.rationale: Without the pragma or Babel plugin, the css prop is not recognized by the JSX transform and silently falls back to a plain style attribute, producing no error but no Emotion styles.

rule.statement: Define reusable style fragments as css() objects at module scope, then compose them via template literal interpolation.
rule.rationale: Composing at module scope avoids re-creating style objects on every render; composition is idiomatic Emotion and produces stable class names.

rule.statement: For Next.js App Router with Emotion, wrap the tree in a 'use client' CacheProvider component. Emotion runtime is not compatible with React Server Components.
rule.rationale: Without CacheProvider in App Router, Emotion's runtime cannot inject styles server-side; FOUC occurs on first paint and hydration mismatches appear in the console.
